requirements: Provide a 3U CubeSat primary structure frame.
Include deployable rail guide grooves for CDS spec.
Support PC/104 stack with standoffs.

Maintain electrical connectivity for onboard systems.
Facilitate thermal management for heat dissipation.
Enable mechanical deployment for CubeSat release.
Provide service access for maintenance.

generated_parts.label: Integration Harness + Datum Spine
generated_parts.spec: Integration_harness closure spine with harness_channel, connector_keying, assembly_datum pads, routing clips, service hatch, reciprocal interface labels, inspection checklist marks

requirements: Inter-board harness routing
Connectors, assembly datums, connection types and load-path closure
Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown
Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels
Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover
Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges
Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating
Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings, spar sockets, or mating pads are visible and labeled
Critical slip, snap, pin, hinge, and slide interfaces state plausible clearances
Each subsystem has at least three adjacent interfaces or datums
Fits are named with standard intent: clearance fit for sliding, transition for locating, interference/press only where justified, with a numeric allowance
Holes, slots, and pockets match real catalog hardware sizes (M3/M4 clearance holes, standard bearing OD, common pin diameters)
Material/process, wall assumptions, build direction, and supports are explicit enough for design review
Verify/risk items reference real features or interfaces from part_tree/geometry_ops
Risks cover blocked access, weak interfaces, tolerance mismatch, process limitations, and unsafe overclaiming
Each stated requirement maps to a verification method (inspection, demonstration, analysis, test) tied to a named feature or interface
Cross-subsystem interfaces state owner, mating geometry, and what is exchanged (load, power, data, fluid)

Every inter-part interface names a specific connection type (bolted, threaded_insert, snap_fit, press_fit, dovetail_slide, clevis_pin, revolute_bearing, band_clamp, adhesive_lap, o_ring, connector_mate, harness_passthrough) with its DOF and a numeric clearance

Parts can be installed in a stated order where every fastener, connector, and mating feature stays reachable; no installed part blocks access to a later one; last-installed and captive parts are identified
One primary datum part anchors the assembly; each other part locates off a named parent datum using a 3-2-1 scheme, so tolerance does not accumulate uncontrolled across the stack
Every part's primary load traces through named interfaces to the base/ground; the load path closes for all parts and high-load joints use load-appropriate connection types

Compared to the previous version the design moves the right direction: fewer interfaces, parts consolidated where service is preserved, open load paths closed, and redundancy added only at critical joints
The assembly uses the fewest interfaces that still allow service and manufacture; each remaining interface is justified by service, motion, manufacture split, or load transfer

verify.value: All required features are present.
verify.rule: Each required evidence_feature must appear in part_tree/geometry_ops.

verify.value: Estimated mass is within acceptable limits.
verify.rule: The subsystem must adhere to the overall mass budget.

verify.value: All primary requirements map to specific geometry.
verify.rule: Each stated requirement must map to a verification method tied to a named feature or interface.

verify.value: Interfaces are reciprocal and consistent.
verify.rule: Cross-subsystem interfaces must state owner, mating geometry, and what is exchanged.

verify.value: Visible locking or preload evidence is present.
verify.rule: Critical joints need visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence.

risk.desc: Potential for harness strain relief failure due to inadequate bend radius.
risk.mit: Ensure all cables follow specified minimum bend radius and use strain relief features.

risk.desc: Risk of mis-mating connectors due to insufficient keying.
risk.mit: Implement robust keying features on all connectors.

risk.desc: Possible alignment issues if datum pads are not clearly visible.
risk.mit: Ensure datum pads are easily identifiable and labeled.

risk.desc: Risk of assembly errors if clearance values are not accurately defined.
risk.mit: Specify all critical clearances with precise measurements.

risk.desc: Potential for non-standard fits leading to improper assembly.
risk.mit: Use standard fit vocabulary for all mating interfaces.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Inter-board harness routing, connectors, assembly datums, connection types and load-path closure

physics_paths.description: Data and power routing from central controller to various sensors
Access for maintenance and reconfiguration of harness and connectors

internal_feature_targets.description: Channel for routing harnesses between modules
Keying features on connectors to prevent mis-mating
Datum pads for alignment during assembly
Clips to secure harnesses and prevent movement

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary datum on the central PCB controller
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual and tactile inspection for alignment and fit

verification_focus.requirement: Harness strain relief and bend radius
